title: Meridian ESG Impact Bond Fund
content:
The Meridian ESG Impact Bond Fund (ISIN: LU2847392851) is a Luxembourg-domiciled SICAV designed to provide investors with sustainable fixed income exposure while promoting environmental and social characteristics in accordance with SFDR Article 8 classification. The fund maintains a diversified portfolio of investment-grade and high-yield corporate bonds, sovereign debt, and supranational securities from issuers demonstrating strong ESG credentials and measurable positive impact outcomes.

Fund Objectives and Strategy: The fund seeks to generate attractive risk-adjusted returns through active management of a globally diversified bond portfolio with duration targeting 4-6 years. Our investment process integrates comprehensive ESG analysis with traditional credit research, focusing on sectors aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals including renewable energy infrastructure, social housing, healthcare innovation, and sustainable agriculture. The fund excludes investments in fossil fuels, tobacco, weapons manufacturing, and entities with significant controversies based on our proprietary ESG scoring methodology.

Performance and Risk Metrics: As of the last reporting period, the fund achieved a 12-month net return of 4.2% (EUR hedged share class), outperforming the Bloomberg Global Aggregate Bond Index by 180 basis points. The fund maintains a modified duration of 5.1 years with an average credit quality of BBB+. Geographic allocation comprises 45% European issuers, 30% North American, 15% Asia-Pacific, and 10% emerging markets. Sector concentration limits ensure no single sector exceeds 25% of total assets, with current largest allocations to renewable energy (18%), healthcare (16%), and financial services (14%).

MiFID II Suitability Assessment: Under MiFID II Article 25 requirements, this product is suitable for clients seeking moderate risk exposure with investment horizons exceeding three years. The fund targets investors with basic knowledge of bond markets and ESG investing principles. Minimum investment thresholds apply: €10,000 for retail shares (Class R), €500,000 for institutional shares (Class I). Annual management fees range from 0.65% (Class I) to 1.25% (Class R), with performance fees capped at 10% of outperformance above benchmark.

ESG Integration and Impact Measurement: Our ESG research team conducts quarterly impact assessments using third-party data from MSCI ESG Research and Sustainalytics. The fund maintains weighted average ESG scores in the top quartile of comparable strategies. Impact metrics include estimated CO2 emissions avoided (currently 125,000 tonnes annually), renewable energy capacity financed (450 MW), and social impact beneficiaries (approximately 2.3 million individuals through healthcare and education financing). Principal adverse impact indicators are monitored monthly with detailed reporting provided semi-annually.

Risk Considerations: Key risks include credit risk from potential issuer defaults, interest rate sensitivity affecting bond valuations, and ESG methodology risks where sustainability criteria may limit investment universe or concentrate exposures. Currency hedging is employed for EUR base currency shares, though residual currency exposure may remain. The fund's focus on ESG leaders may result in sector or geographic concentrations that could underperform broader market indices during certain periods.

Regulatory Status: This fund promotes environmental and social characteristics under SFDR Article 8 but does not commit to sustainable investment as primary objective. Pre-contractual and periodic disclosures are available on our website. The fund is authorized for distribution across EU member states under UCITS directive and maintains relevant regulatory approvals for institutional and retail client access through Meridian Wealth Partners' distribution network.
